title: Toehold switches achieve >400-fold average ON/OFF dynamic range

leaf.formula: ON expression ÷ OFF expression (average across a toehold switch library)

title: Synthetic transcriptional logic gates have ON/OFF dynamic range on the order of 10-100x

leaf.formula: ON expression ÷ OFF expression (dynamic range D) of a synthetic transcriptional gate

schema_fit.constraint: Chapter states an order-of-magnitude range (10-100x), not a point measurement.
schema_fit.current_ir_behavior: Manifest schema requires a single value+uncertainty pair, forcing an arithmetic midpoint (55±45) onto what the chapter frames as a multiplicative/log-scale span.
schema_fit.candidate_resolution: Add optional value_min/value_max fields (or a log-scale uncertainty representation) to the quantity leaf for order-of-magnitude claims.
schema_fit.purity_cost: An arithmetic midpoint is not the geometric center implied by a 10-100x range, so the encoded value slightly misrepresents the claim's shape though it stays within the stated bounds.

title: The practical ceiling for cascaded genetic logic gates is 3-4 layers deep

schema_fit.constraint: Chapter states a discrete range (3-4 layers), not a single measured depth.
schema_fit.current_ir_behavior: Forces the range into a continuous value=3.5±0.5.
schema_fit.candidate_resolution: Represent as an inclusive integer range [3,4] rather than a continuous value+uncertainty pair, since gate count is discrete.
schema_fit.purity_cost: A continuous midpoint (3.5 layers) implies a precision the discrete claim doesn't have — a circuit cannot have 3.5 gate layers.

title: HLA-A*02 loss of heterozygosity occurs in ~10-20% of many solid tumors

schema_fit.constraint: Chapter gives a pooled range (~10-20%) across 'many solid tumors' without specifying which tumor types or a per-indication estimate.
schema_fit.current_ir_behavior: Collapses to a single point value (15%±5%) with no tumor-type context.
schema_fit.candidate_resolution: Tag with per-tumor-type context (context.condition) once a per-indication breakdown is available, rather than one pooled figure.
schema_fit.purity_cost: A single prevalence figure obscures that LOH rate is tumor-type-dependent, which is exactly the clinical nuance a stratified context field would need to capture.

title: Cascaded genetic logic gates degrade rather than sharpen because per-gate errors compound multiplicatively with depth

leaf.data: Deep digital logic cascades in cells are fragile: because biology has no signal-restoring step analogous to a transistor pinning to rail voltages, each added genetic gate's error compounds onto the last, so effective classifier margin shrinks rather than staying constant with depth.
leaf.warrant: With per-gate ON/OFF dynamic range D~10-100x and per-gate error rate ε of a few percent, the effective signal contrast across k series gates falls roughly as the product of per-stage contrasts and error accumulates super-linearly, making 3-4 layers the practical ceiling before OFF-state leak floods the output — the chapter's stated reason every clinically advanced logic-gated cell therapy uses only two, at most three, inputs.

title: Wiring a downstream circuit module to an upstream one loads and changes the upstream module's behavior (retroactivity)

leaf.data: In synthetic gene circuits, connecting a downstream module to an upstream one is not free: the downstream binding sites sequester upstream signal, changing the upstream module's own behavior — the biomolecular analog of electrical impedance/fan-out loading.
leaf.warrant: Formalized by Del Vecchio, Ninfa & Sontag as retroactivity in modular cell biology, and compounded by resource competition (circuits sharing ribosomes, RNA polymerase, and amino acids throttle each other), so a wired circuit is never the clean, independent-gate diagram drawn on a whiteboard.

title: Robust perfect adaptation in a synthetic circuit requires integral feedback, minimally realized as antithetic integral control

leaf.data: Holding a circuit's output at a fixed setpoint despite arbitrary, persistent disturbances and parameter variation (robust perfect adaptation) requires integral feedback; the minimal biomolecular implementation is the antithetic integral feedback motif, in which two controller species mutually annihilate so that their difference integrates the error.
leaf.warrant: This is a control-theory necessity result imported into synthetic biology by Khammash and colleagues (Briat, Gupta & Khammash, Cell Systems 2016) and subsequently realized as a working mammalian proportional-integral feedback circuit (Frei et al., PNAS 2022), meaning a therapeutic circuit that must hold a precise output in a variable in-vivo environment cannot rely on open-loop tuning.

title: A NOT-gate keyed to a genomic loss event (LOH) is more escape-resistant than one keyed to antigen gain

leaf.data: Keying a logic-gated cell therapy's inhibitory (NOT) input to a somatic genomic deletion — such as loss of the germline HLA-A*02 allele via loss of heterozygosity — is more escape-resistant than keying it to an expressed antigen, because a tumor cannot re-express what it has irreversibly deleted, unlike antigen-loss escape from an activating CAR.
leaf.warrant: A2 Bio's Tmod platform implements kill = activator (CEA/mesothelin/EGFR) AND NOT HLA-A*02: normal cells retain both HLA-A*02 copies and are protected by the veto, while tumor cells that underwent HLA-A*02 LOH lose the veto and are killed; this logic is in clinical testing as A2B530 (EVEREST-1, NCT05736731), A2B694, and A2B395.

title: Narrative: logic-gated cell therapies moving from bacterial reporter demos into the clinic
Narrative: circuit topology, not the antigen binder, is becoming the defensible IP in cell therapy
